title: tanacetum atkinsonii
intro: Tanacetum atkinsonii, also known as Atkinson's tansy or Atkinson's pyrethrum, is a flowering plant species native to the Himalayan region. It belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to repel insects and other pests. The plant's leaves and flowers contain compounds such as pyrethrins, which have been used in herbal preparations to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Tanacetum atkinsonii, such as teas and infusions, have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and respiratory issues, while also providing antiseptic and anti-inflammatory benefits.
